title: Claude Can Now Control Your Computer (This Changes Everything)

eval.summary: Short-form promotional video covering Claude's computer use feature (GUI automation via screen vision + mouse/keyboard control) and 'dispatch' (remote mobile-to-desktop task execution). Content is surface-level marketing with no technical depth — no API details, no architecture, no token efficiency data, no integration patterns. The 'dispatch' framing is essentially Anthropic's Claude app controlling a desktop session remotely, which is a wrapper over the same computer use API already available to Veritas via Claude API. Alan OS already has Windows automation via COM and n8n orchestration. Computer use API is on Anthropic — the existing stack's LLM provider — but the video provides zero implementation detail, no webhook/API schema, no latency or cost benchmarks, and no discussion of how to integrate programmatically. The lead magnet CTA ('comment WTF') confirms this is top-of-funnel content farming, not a technical reference. No new tools, repos, or techniques surfaced beyond what Anthropic's own docs cover.
eval.fit_rationale: Claude computer use is on the existing LLM provider and could theoretically extend Alan OS Windows automation, but this video contributes nothing actionable toward that integration.

stack_evaluation.replaces_or_complements: nothing — Alan OS already owns Windows automation via Outlook COM and n8n; computer use API would complement but requires Anthropic docs, not this video
stack_evaluation.reasoning: Fails P3 entirely — no token efficiency data, no eval rubric, no before/after comparison on any task set. Fails P7 — computer use overlaps directly with existing Alan OS Windows automation layer (COM, n8n, FastAPI) at well above 40% domain overlap, and this video provides no BUILD_OR_EXTEND analysis or justification for a new integration path. Content is promotional fluff with a lead magnet CTA; zero implementation signal.
